title: else & else if 🔀
description: Handle multiple choices and alternative paths.

slides.title: Plan B 🛡️
slides.content:
An `if` statement tells the code what to do when a condition is true. But what if it's false?

We use the `else` keyword to provide a "fallback" or "Plan B" block of code.

slides.title: Using else
slides.content: An `else` block MUST come immediately after an `if` block's closing brace `}`.

quizData.question: When does the code inside an `else` block execute?

options.text: Always, right after the if block.
Only when the attaching `if` condition evaluates to False.

quizData.explanation: The `else` block is the guaranteed alternative. It ONLY runs if the `if` condition fails.

slides.title: Multiple Choices 🔀
slides.content: Sometimes there aren't just two options. If you need to check multiple specific conditions in a row, you can chain them using `else if`.

slides.title: Chaining else if
slides.content: C will check each condition from top to bottom. **The moment it finds a True one, it runs that block and completely skips the rest!**

quizData.question: In the grading example above, if `score` is 85, why doesn't it also print 'Grade: C' (since 85 is technically >= 70)?

options.text: C evaluates all of them, but hides the later prints.
An if/else chain stops checking the moment it hits the first True condition.

quizData.explanation: Because `score >= 80` was True, it executed the 'B' block and then completely exited the entire chain.
